_comment: Issue #60 — HillClimbing loop strategy cross-language replay fixtures. Each scenario scripts a metric_sequence (element 0 is the iteration-0 BASELINE, measured with NO agent turn; status kept; duration = the baseline evaluator-call time only). Element index i (i>=1) is the metric AFTER iteration i's agent turn. A null element is a crash (counts as a non-improvement; metric_value EMPTY in the TSV). The payload `direction` is authoritative for the keep/revert decision via should_keep. All scripted MetricResults carry a ZERO duration, so every duration_secs renders as 0.000000 — this lets expected_tsv be asserted byte-identically across languages.

scenarios.name: keep_then_regress_stagnation_halt
scenarios._doc: maximize: baseline 1.0 (kept), iter1 2.0 (kept, improve), iter2 1.5 (discarded, regress vs 2.0). max_stagnation 1 halts after the first non-improvement. best_metric stays 2.0.
scenarios.expected_tsv:
iteration	commit_hash	metric_value	direction	status	duration_secs	description
0		1.000000	maximize	kept	0.000000	scripted metric
1		2.000000	maximize	kept	0.000000	scripted metric
2		1.500000	maximize	discarded	0.000000	scripted metric

scenarios.name: stagnation_halt_three
scenarios._doc: maximize: baseline 5.0 (kept), then three regresses to 4.0. max_stagnation 3 halts after the third consecutive non-improvement. best stays 5.0.

scenarios.name: stagnation_reset_on_improve
scenarios._doc: maximize: baseline 1.0 (kept), iter1 0.5 (discard), iter2 0.5 (discard, stagnation=2), iter3 2.0 (kept, IMPROVE -> stagnation resets to 0), iter4 1.0 (discard, stagnation=1). With max_stagnation 3 and max_turns 4 the run ends on the turn budget, never hitting the stagnation cap.

scenarios.name: crash_counts_as_non_improvement
scenarios._doc: maximize: baseline 1.0 (kept), iter1 crash (null) -> non-improvement, metric_value EMPTY, status crashed. max_stagnation 1 halts.
scenarios.expected_tsv:
iteration	commit_hash	metric_value	direction	status	duration_secs	description
0		1.000000	maximize	kept	0.000000	scripted metric
1			maximize	crashed	0.000000	scripted metric

scenarios.name: revert_on_regress
scenarios._doc: minimize (lower is better): baseline 2.0 (kept), iter1 3.0 (worse -> discard). revert_on_no_improvement true issues exactly one git reset --hard HEAD. max_stagnation 1 halts.

scenarios.name: revert_off_regress
scenarios._doc: minimize: baseline 2.0 (kept), iter1 3.0 (worse -> discard). revert_on_no_improvement false -> NO git reset. max_stagnation 1 halts.

scenarios.name: strict_min_delta_boundary
scenarios._doc: minimize: baseline 2.0 (kept), iter1 1.5 with min_improvement_delta 0.5. An improvement of EXACTLY min_delta is NOT progress -> discarded. max_stagnation 1 halts; best stays 2.0.
